vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the current visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/unidentifiable. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by a single, potentially detached leaf fragment near the rabbit anchor.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Critical Status: Only a single leaf fragment remains near the rabbit anchor.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface, no growth detected.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface, no growth detected.
plant_audit.P3: Single leaf remnant; appears dehydrated or detached.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface, no growth detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dark and potentially saturated, but lacks any vegetative cover.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of eggshell fragments on the soil surface of the black pot.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant biological growth outside of pots.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows a single green stem/leaf in the black pot.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Stem appears to be wilting or losing turgor pressure.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Total loss of visible plant structure; the biome has collapsed into a state of bare soil and debris.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of total failure. The lack of photosynthetic material suggests the specimens have either been removed or have undergone complete senescence/necrosis.
anomalies: Eggshell fragments present in the black pot (Intervention).
Complete absence of expected flora (Systemic Loss).
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic decline in the biome. Starting from a state where at least one specimen (P3) was visible, the sequence documents a rapid loss of plant matter. By the current timestamp, the pots are effectively barren, containing only soil and non-biological debris (eggshells). The environment appears to be devoid of living plant life.
